perturbation.original_text: The information disclosed by Discloser to NIST under this Agreement is [detailed, non -proprietary description of proprietary information] (“Proprietary Information”). The following NIST ’s representative (s) will be receiving Proprietary Information : [List the names of the NIST Employees]. NIST shall not disclose the Proprietary Information to any of its employees other than those who have a need to review it and which employees are legally obligated to honor the confidentiality provisions herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The information disclosed by Discloser to NIST under this Agreement is [detailed, non -proprietary description of proprietary information] (“Proprietary Data”). The following NIST ’s representative (s) will be receiving Proprietary Information : [List the names of the NIST Employees]. NIST shall not disclose the Proprietary Information to any of its employees other than those who have a need to review it and which employees are legally obligated to honor the confidentiality provisions herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changed 'Proprietary Information' to 'Proprietary Data' in the first sentence of Section 1. This creates confusion as the rest of the section and document still refers to 'Proprietary Information', leading to uncertainty about which term applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The information disclosed by Discloser to NIST under this Agreement is [detailed, non -proprietary description of proprietary information] (“Proprietary Data”). The following NIST ’s representative (s) will be receiving Proprietary Information : [List the names of the NIST Employees]. NIST shall not disclose the Proprietary Information to any of its employees other than those who have a need to review it and which employees are legally obligated to honor the confidentiality provisions herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text consistently refers to 'Proprietary Information'. The changed text alters the first instance of 'Proprietary Information' to 'Proprietary Data'. The contradicted text section also contains a reference to both 'Proprietary Data' and 'Proprietary Information', the rest of the contract, including the changed section, consistently refers to 'Proprietary Information'. The perturbation explanation states this creates confusion because the document refers to 'Proprietary Information'. This inconsistency between 'Proprietary Data' and 'Proprietary Information' creates a direct conflict regarding the definition and scope of information covered by the agreement, thus, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
